files.text: sources/comments/files/20251121-5242/Joint Consumer Advocates (PA, DE) Comments - Large Loads ANOPR RM26-4.txt

summary: Speaking for utility consumers, the joint advocates back the reform but center residential ratepayers: data-center load should pay its own way through strong cost causation and transparency, not be subsidized by households.
key_points: Cost causation so large loads bear the costs they impose.
Transparency for states to keep data-center costs off retail customers.
Guard against stranded-cost risk if forecast load does not materialize.
